<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Variational methods</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

Variational methods

Stationarity is the universal pose: every classical equation of motion, every minimal surface, every gauge field is the critical point of an energy. The calculus of variations turns "minimise" into a differential equation (Euler–Lagrange), then into an existence theorem (the direct method on a reflexive Banach space), then into the saddle-point yoga of mountain-pass and Morse theory.

blocks.html:
1. Functional derivative

A functional $J\colon X\to\mathbb{R}$ on a Banach space $X$ assigns a scalar to each function. The classic example is the Dirichlet energy

$$J(u) \;=\; \tfrac12\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx \qquad (u\in H^1_0(\Omega)).$$

To minimise $J$ we need a notion of "derivative". Two upgrades of the directional derivative live in infinite dimensions.

Gateaux: directional derivative

Fix $u\in X$ and $h\in X$. The Gateaux derivative in direction $h$ is

$$\langle J'(u), h\rangle \;=\; \lim_{\varepsilon\to 0}\frac{J(u+\varepsilon h)-J(u)}{\varepsilon}.$$

If this limit exists and is linear and continuous in $h$, we call $J'(u)\in X^*$ the Gateaux differential. Concretely for the Dirichlet energy,

$$\langle J'(u), h\rangle \;=\; \int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot \nabla h\,dx,$$

obtained by expanding $|\nabla(u+\varepsilon h)|^2 = |\nabla u|^2 + 2\varepsilon\nabla u\cdot\nabla h + \varepsilon^2|\nabla h|^2$ and reading off the linear coefficient.

Fréchet: uniform-in-direction

$J$ is Fréchet differentiable at $u$ if there exists a bounded linear $J'(u)\in X^*$ with

$$J(u+h) \;=\; J(u) + \langle J'(u),h\rangle + o(\|h\|), \qquad \|h\|\to 0.$$

The error term is uniform across all directions. Fréchet implies Gateaux; the converse fails — even on $\mathbb{R}^2$ the function with sharp ridges along radial lines can have all directional derivatives but no Fréchet differential. For the smooth functionals of physics the two coincide.

In physics literature $\delta J/\delta u(x)$ denotes the density: the function for which $\langle J'(u),h\rangle = \int (\delta J/\delta u)\,h\,dx$. For the Dirichlet energy this density is $-\Delta u$ (after one integration by parts).

<script>
/* ============================ §1 functional-derivative widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#fdiff-svg"), pSel = $("#fdiff-p"),
        eS = $("#fdiff-eps"), eO = $("#fdiff-eps-out"),
        out = $("#fdiff-out");
  const W=580, H=320;
  // top panel: u and u+eps*h vs x in [0,1]
  const t={x0:46, y0:18, w:W-66, h:160};
  // bottom panel: J(eps) vs eps in [-1,1]
  const b={x0:46, y0:200, w:W-66, h:100};

  function u(x){ return 1 + Math.sin(Math.PI*x); }
  function h(x){ return Math.sin(2*Math.PI*x); }
  function J(p, eps){
    const N=400; let s=0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const x = (i+0.5)/N;
      s += Math.pow(u(x)+eps*h(x), p);
    }
    return s/N;
  }
  function dJ(p){
    // <J'(u),h> = p ∫ u^{p-1} h dx
    const N=400; let s=0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const x = (i+0.5)/N;
      s += p*Math.pow(u(x), p-1)*h(x);
    }
    return s/N;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const p = parseFloat(pSel.value);
    const eps = parseFloat(eS.value);
    eO.textContent = `ε = ${eps.toFixed(2)}`;

    // ===== top panel =====
    const sxT = x => t.x0 + x*t.w;
    const ymin=-0.5, ymax=2.6;
    const syT = y => t.y0 + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*t.h;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:t.x0,y:t.y0,width:t.w,height:t.h,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:t.x0,y1:syT(0),x2:t.x0+t.w,y2:syT(0),stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-dasharray":"2,3"}));
    // u(x)
    let pts=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){ const x=i/200; pts.push([sxT(x), syT(u(x))]); }
    let pd = pts.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pd, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":2}));
    // u + eps*h(x)
    pts=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){ const x=i/200; pts.push([sxT(x), syT(u(x)+eps*h(x))]); }
    pd = pts.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pd, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2}));
    // labels
    const lblU = SVG("text",{x:t.x0+8, y:t.y0+14,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    lblU.textContent = "u(x)"; svg.appendChild(lblU);
    const lblP = SVG("text",{x:t.x0+8, y:t.y0+30,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    lblP.textContent = "u + ε h"; svg.appendChild(lblP);

    // ===== bottom panel: J(eps) =====
    const sxB = e => b.x0 + (e+1)/2*b.w;
    const Jvals=[]; let jmin=Infinity, jmax=-Infinity;
    for(let i=0;i<=120;i++){
      const e = -1 + 2*i/120;
      const v = J(p, e);
      Jvals.push([e,v]);
      if(v<jmin) jmin=v; if(v>jmax) jmax=v;
    }
    const dy = (jmax-jmin) || 1;
    const yLo = jmin - 0.05*dy, yHi = jmax + 0.05*dy;
    const syB = y => b.y0 + (yHi-y)/(yHi-yLo)*b.h;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:b.x0,y:b.y0,width:b.w,height:b.h,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // x-axis at eps=0
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sxB(0),y1:b.y0,x2:sxB(0),y2:b.y0+b.h,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-dasharray":"2,3"}));
    // J curve
    const jpts = Jvals.map(([e,v])=>[sxB(e), syB(v)]);
    const jpd = jpts.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:jpd, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":2}));
    // linear approximation: J(u) + eps * <J'(u),h>
    const J0 = J(p, 0), slope = dJ(p);
    const linPts = [-1,1].map(e => [sxB(e), syB(J0 + e*slope)]);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:linPts[0][0],y1:linPts[0][1],x2:linPts[1][0],y2:linPts[1][1],
      stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":1.6,"stroke-dasharray":"4,4"}));
    // marker at current eps
    const cx = sxB(eps), cy = syB(J(p, eps));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy,r:5,fill:"var(--pink)"}));
    // current linear approx
    const cyLin = syB(J0 + eps*slope);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy:cyLin,r:4,fill:"var(--cyan)"}));

    // labels
    const lJ = SVG("text",{x:b.x0+8, y:b.y0+14,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    lJ.textContent = "J(u + ε h)"; svg.appendChild(lJ);
    const lL = SVG("text",{x:b.x0+8, y:b.y0+30,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    lL.textContent = "J(u) + ε ⟨J'(u),h⟩"; svg.appendChild(lL);
    // axis tick labels
    for(const e of [-1,-0.5,0,0.5,1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sxB(e),y1:b.y0+b.h,x2:sxB(e),y2:b.y0+b.h+4,stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const tk = SVG("text",{x:sxB(e),y:b.y0+b.h+16,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      tk.textContent = e.toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(tk);
    }
    const xlbl = SVG("text",{x:b.x0+b.w/2, y:b.y0+b.h+30, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xlbl.textContent = "ε"; svg.appendChild(xlbl);

    const Jcur = J(p,eps), lin = J0 + eps*slope, gap = Jcur - lin;
    out.textContent = `J(u) = ${J0.toFixed(4)}    ⟨J'(u),h⟩ = ${slope.toFixed(4)}    J(u+εh) = ${Jcur.toFixed(4)}    linear = ${lin.toFixed(4)}    gap = ${gap.toFixed(4)} (= O(ε²))`;
  }
  pSel.addEventListener("change", render);
  eS.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Euler–Lagrange equations

If $u_*$ minimises $J(u)=\int_a^b L(x,u,u')\,dx$ over functions with $u(a)=\alpha$, $u(b)=\beta$, then $\langle J'(u_*),h\rangle = 0$ for every test variation $h$ vanishing at the endpoints. Compute:

$$\langle J'(u),h\rangle \;=\; \int_a^b \big[\partial_u L(x,u,u')\,h + \partial_{u'}L(x,u,u')\,h'\big]\,dx.$$

Integrating the second term by parts (boundary contributions vanish because $h(a)=h(b)=0$) gives

$$\langle J'(u),h\rangle \;=\; \int_a^b \Big[\partial_u L - \tfrac{d}{dx}\partial_{u'}L\Big]\,h\,dx.$$

By the fundamental lemma of the calculus of variations, vanishing for all admissible $h$ forces the bracket to be zero pointwise:

$$\boxed{\;\frac{d}{dx}\,\partial_{u'}L(x,u,u') \;=\; \partial_u L(x,u,u').\;}$$

This is the Euler–Lagrange equation — the necessary condition for a classical extremum.

The brachistochrone

Bernoulli's 1696 challenge: find the curve $y(x)$ from $(0,0)$ to $(x_1,y_1)$ down which a bead slides under gravity in least time. With $v=\sqrt{2gy}$ from energy conservation,

$$T(y) \;=\; \int_0^{x_1}\!\! \frac{\sqrt{1+(y')^2}}{\sqrt{2gy}}\,dx.$$

The Lagrangian $L=\sqrt{1+(y')^2}/\sqrt{2gy}$ has no explicit $x$-dependence, so the Beltrami identity $L - y'\partial_{y'}L = C$ holds. Solving gives the cycloid

$$x(\theta) \;=\; r(\theta - \sin\theta), \qquad y(\theta) \;=\; r(1-\cos\theta),$$

traced by a point on a wheel of radius $r$ rolling on a horizontal line.

Brachistochrone vs straight line vs parabola
drag the slider to race; lowest time wins
t = 0.00
Three curves connect $(0,0)$ to $(2,1)$ under gravity $g=1$. Cycloid (yellow) wins by Bernoulli's analysis; line (cyan) is shortest path but slowest; parabola (violet) is intermediate. Each bead travels at its instantaneous speed $\sqrt{2gy}$ — the deeper, faster.

See also
  • Lᵖ spaces

<script>
/* ============================ §2 brachistochrone widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#brach-svg"), tS = $("#brach-t"), tO = $("#brach-t-out"),
        out = $("#brach-out");
  const W=580, H=320, padL=40, padR=20, padT=20, padB=40;
  // domain: x∈[0,2.2], y∈[0,1.4] (y down)
  const xmin=0, xmax=2.2, ymin=0, ymax=1.4;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (y-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  // Solve for cycloid through (2,1). Parametrise x=r(θ-sinθ), y=r(1-cosθ).
  // Numerically: find r,θ_max with r(θ-sinθ)=2 AND r(1-cosθ)=1. Solve as a system:
  // ratio (θ-sinθ)/(1-cosθ) = 2; bisection on θ.
  function solveCycloid(){
    let lo=0.01, hi=Math.PI*1.99;
    for(let i=0;i<60;i++){
      const m=(lo+hi)/2;
      const ratio = (m-Math.sin(m))/(1-Math.cos(m));
      if(ratio<2) lo=m; else hi=m;
    }
    const theta_end = (lo+hi)/2;
    const r = 1/(1-Math.cos(theta_end));
    return {r, theta_end};
  }
  const cyc = solveCycloid();

  function cycloid(s){ // s in [0,1] arc-progress in θ
    const th = s*cyc.theta_end;
    return [cyc.r*(th-Math.sin(th)), cyc.r*(1-Math.cos(th))];
  }
  function line(s){ return [2*s, 1*s]; }
  function parabola(s){ const x=2*s; return [x, (x*x)/4]; }

  // travel time along curve y(x) under gravity: T = ∫ sqrt((dx² + dy²)/(2gy)) dt
  // numerical: fine sample, accumulate ds/v
  function timeOf(curve){
    const N=2000, g=1;
    let T=0, prev=curve(1e-5);
    for(let i=1;i<=N;i++){
      const s=i/N;
      const cur = curve(s);
      const dx = cur[0]-prev[0], dy=cur[1]-prev[1];
      const ds = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
      const ymid = 0.5*(prev[1]+cur[1]);
      const v = Math.sqrt(2*g*Math.max(ymid, 1e-6));
      T += ds/v;
      prev = cur;
    }
    return T;
  }
  // arc-length parametrisation of bead position at time t∈[0,T_curve]
  function beadAt(curve, t, Ttotal){
    const N=2000, g=1;
    let T=0, prev=curve(1e-5);
    for(let i=1;i<=N;i++){
      const s=i/N;
      const cur = curve(s);
      const dx = cur[0]-prev[0], dy=cur[1]-prev[1];
      const ds = Math.hypot(dx,dy);
      const ymid = 0.5*(prev[1]+cur[1]);
      const v = Math.sqrt(2*g*Math.max(ymid, 1e-6));
      const dt = ds/v;
      if(T+dt >= t){
        const frac = (t-T)/dt;
        return [prev[0]+frac*dx, prev[1]+frac*dy];
      }
      T += dt; prev=cur;
    }
    return prev;
  }

  const Tcyc = timeOf(cycloid), Tlin = timeOf(line), Tpar = timeOf(parabola);
  const Tmax = Math.max(Tcyc, Tlin, Tpar);

  function drawCurve(curve, color, dash){
    const pts=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const c = curve(i/200);
      pts.push([sx(c[0]), sy(c[1])]);
    }
    const d = pts.map((p,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${p[0]} ${p[1]}`).join(" ");
    const path = SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:color, "stroke-width":2});
    if(dash) path.setAttribute("stroke-dasharray", dash);
    svg.appendChild(path);
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const tNorm = parseFloat(tS.value);
    const tReal = tNorm * Tmax; // real time
    tO.textContent = `t = ${tReal.toFixed(3)}`;

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL, y:padT, width:W-padL-padR, height:H-padT-padB,
      fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // y-down axis label
    const yL = SVG("text",{x:padL-22, y:padT+8,"font-size":11, fill:"var(--mute)"});
    yL.textContent = "y"; svg.appendChild(yL);
    // start, end markers
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(0), cy:sy(0), r:5, fill:"var(--green)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(2), cy:sy(1), r:5, fill:"var(--pink)"}));
    const ls = SVG("text",{x:sx(0)+8, y:sy(0)-8,"font-size":11, fill:"var(--green)"});
    ls.textContent = "start"; svg.appendChild(ls);
    const le = SVG("text",{x:sx(2)+8, y:sy(1)-8,"font-size":11, fill:"var(--pink)"});
    le.textContent = "end"; svg.appendChild(le);

    drawCurve(cycloid, "var(--yellow)", null);
    drawCurve(line, "var(--cyan)", "4,4");
    drawCurve(parabola, "var(--violet)", "2,3");

    // animated beads
    function bead(curve, color, Ttotal){
      if(tReal > Ttotal){
        // arrived
        const pos = curve(1);
        svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(pos[0]), cy:sy(pos[1]), r:6, fill:color, opacity:"0.5"}));
        return true;
      }
      const pos = beadAt(curve, tReal, Ttotal);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(pos[0]), cy:sy(pos[1]), r:6, fill:color}));
      return false;
    }
    const arrived = [
      bead(cycloid, "var(--yellow)", Tcyc),
      bead(line, "var(--cyan)", Tlin),
      bead(parabola, "var(--violet)", Tpar)
    ];

    // legend top-right
    const lx = W-padR-150, ly = padT+12;
    const items = [
      ["cycloid", "var(--yellow)", Tcyc],
      ["line",    "var(--cyan)",   Tlin],
      ["parabola","var(--violet)", Tpar]
    ];
    items.forEach(([nm,c,T],i)=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:lx,y1:ly+i*16,x2:lx+18,y2:ly+i*16,stroke:c,"stroke-width":2}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:lx+24, y:ly+i*16+4,"font-size":11, fill:c});
      t.textContent = `${nm}  T=${T.toFixed(3)}`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    out.textContent = `cycloid T=${Tcyc.toFixed(4)}   line T=${Tlin.toFixed(4)}   parabola T=${Tpar.toFixed(4)}   ⇒ cycloid wins by ${(100*(Tlin-Tcyc)/Tlin).toFixed(2)}% over the straight line.`;
  }
  tS.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

3. Direct method (Tonelli)

Euler–Lagrange is a necessary condition: every smooth minimiser solves it, but a solution to the EL equation might be a saddle, a local maximum, or non-existent. The direct method proves existence of a minimiser by sequential compactness, three ingredients:

  1. Coercivity: $J(u_k)\to\infty$ whenever $\|u_k\|\to\infty$. Equivalently, sublevel sets $\{J\le c\}$ are bounded.
  2. Reflexivity: $X$ is a reflexive Banach space, so by Banach–Alaoglu every bounded sequence has a weakly convergent subsequence $u_{k_j}\rightharpoonup u_*$.
  3. Weak lower-semicontinuity: $u_k\rightharpoonup u\Rightarrow J(u)\le\liminf_{k\to\infty}J(u_k)$.

The argument: take a minimising sequence $u_k$ with $J(u_k)\to\inf J$. Coercivity bounds it; reflexivity extracts $u_{k_j}\rightharpoonup u_*$; lsc gives

$$J(u_*) \;\le\; \liminf_j J(u_{k_j}) \;=\; \inf J,$$

so $u_*$ attains the infimum. Convexity of $J$ in the gradient is the standard sufficient condition for weak lsc on $W^{1,p}$ — the canonical setting where this works.

Why convexity gives lsc

For $J(u)=\int F(\nabla u)\,dx$ with $F$ convex, write $F$ as the supremum of its affine minorants: $F(\xi) = \sup_\ell\,\ell(\xi)$ where $\ell$ are continuous linear functionals. Each $\int\ell(\nabla u)\,dx$ is weakly continuous (linear), and the supremum of weakly continuous functionals is weakly lsc. The classical example is the Dirichlet integral $F(\xi)=|\xi|^2$ — convex, hence weakly lsc on $H^1$.

Direct method — minimising sequence in $H^1_0(0,1)$
add zigzag detail; energy decreases, length increases
k = 0
Minimising the Dirichlet energy $J(u)=\int_0^1 (u')^2$ subject to $u(0)=0$, $u(1)=1$ and constraint $u\ge 0$. The infimum is attained at $u(x)=x$ with $J=1$. The slider shows $u_k(x)=x + \tfrac{1}{k}\sin(k\pi x)$ — a minimising sequence that weakly converges to $u(x)=x$ but strongly oscillates, illustrating why weak (not strong) lsc is the relevant property.

  • Weak solutions and Galerkin approximation

/* ============================ §3 direct-method widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#direct-svg"), kS = $("#direct-k"), kO = $("#direct-k-out"),
        out = $("#direct-out");
  const W=580, H=320, padL=46, padR=20, padT=20, padB=40;
  const xmin=0, xmax=1, ymin=-0.2, ymax=1.3;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  function uk(x, k){
    if(k===0) return x;
    return x + (1/k)*Math.sin(k*Math.PI*x);
  }
  function J_dirichlet(k){
    // u_k' = 1 + π cos(kπx)
    const N=4000;
    let s=0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const x=(i+0.5)/N;
      const up = (k===0) ? 1 : (1 + Math.PI*Math.cos(k*Math.PI*x));
      s += up*up;
    }
    return s/N;
  }
  function length_k(k){
    const N=4000;
    let s=0;
    for(let i=0;i<N;i++){
      const x=(i+0.5)/N;
      const up = (k===0) ? 1 : (1 + Math.PI*Math.cos(k*Math.PI*x));
      s += Math.sqrt(1+up*up);
    }
    return s/N;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const k = parseInt(kS.value);
    kO.textContent = `k = ${k}`;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL,y:padT,width:W-padL-padR,height:H-padT-padB,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:sy(0),x2:W-padR,y2:sy(0),stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-dasharray":"2,3"}));
    // ticks
    for(const xv of [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(xv),y1:H-padB,x2:sx(xv),y2:H-padB+4,stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:sx(xv),y:H-padB+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = xv.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(const yv of [0,0.5,1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL-4,y1:sy(yv),x2:padL,y2:sy(yv),stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:padL-8,y:sy(yv)+4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = yv.toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // weak limit u(x)=x (cyan, dashed)
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(0),y1:sy(0),x2:sx(1),y2:sy(1),
      stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2,"stroke-dasharray":"4,4"}));

    // u_k(x): yellow
    const N=600;
    const pts=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const x=i/N;
      pts.push([sx(x), sy(uk(x,k))]);
    }
    const pd = pts.map((p,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${p[0]} ${p[1]}`).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pd, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.8}));

    const l2 = SVG("text",{x:W-156,y:padT+34,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    l2.textContent = "weak limit u(x)=x"; svg.appendChild(l2);

    const Jk = J_dirichlet(k), Lk = length_k(k);
    const J0 = 1; // Dirichlet of u(x)=x
    out.textContent = `J(u_k) = ∫(u_k')² = ${Jk.toFixed(4)}    arc-length(u_k) = ${Lk.toFixed(4)}\nweak limit J(u) = ${J0.toFixed(4)}    ${k===0?"":(Jk>J0?`J(u) ≤ liminf J(u_k) ✓ (lsc holds)`:"contradiction!")}`;
  }

4. Mountain-pass theorem

Not every critical point is a minimum. The mountain-pass theorem of Ambrosetti–Rabinowitz (1973) detects saddle critical points by topology of sublevel sets.

Mountain-pass theorem. Let $X$ be a Banach space and $J\in C^1(X,\mathbb{R})$ satisfying:
  • $(MP_1)$ $J(0) = 0$ and there exist $\rho,\alpha>0$ with $J(u)\ge\alpha$ for all $\|u\|=\rho$.
  • $(MP_2)$ There exists $u_*\in X$ with $\|u_*\|>\rho$ and $J(u_*)\le 0$.
  • $(PS)$ Palais–Smale: every sequence with $J(u_k)$ bounded and $J'(u_k)\to 0$ has a strongly convergent subsequence.
Then $c = \inf_{\gamma\in\Gamma}\max_{t\in[0,1]} J(\gamma(t))$ is a critical value, where $\Gamma$ is the space of continuous paths from $0$ to $u_*$.

The picture

$J$ has a "valley" at the origin (a local min) surrounded by a ring of altitude $\ge \alpha$ — the mountain ridge. There's a far point $u_*$ at sea level. Any path from $0$ to $u_*$ must climb over the ridge. The lowest such ridge-height is a saddle critical value: there's a critical point at altitude $c$ where two valleys meet through a pass.

Why the Palais–Smale condition? It plays the role of compactness for non-coercive functionals. The deformation lemma normally produces a minimising sequence $u_k$ with $J(u_k)\to c$, $J'(u_k)\to 0$; without (PS), this sequence might escape to infinity instead of converging to a critical point.

Application: nonlinear elliptic equations

For the semilinear problem $-\Delta u = |u|^{p-2}u$ on a bounded domain (with $p<2^*$ subcritical), the energy

$$J(u) \;=\; \tfrac12\int|\nabla u|^2 - \tfrac1p\int|u|^p$$

has a local min at $0$ and a far point $u_*=\lambda v$ for $\lambda$ large where $J(u_*)<0$. Mountain-pass produces a non-trivial weak solution. The semilinear theory of bound states is built on this template.

<script>
/* ============================ §4 mountain-pass widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg = $("#mp-svg"), bS = $("#mp-bend"), bO = $("#mp-bend-out"),
        dS = $("#mp-detour"), dO = $("#mp-detour-out"),
        out = $("#mp-out");
  const W=580, H=380, padL=40, padR=20, padT=20, padB=40;
  const xmin=-1.8, xmax=1.8, ymin=-1.4, ymax=1.4;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  // J(x,y) = (x²-1)² + y² - 0.4x
  function J(x,y){ return (x*x-1)*(x*x-1) + y*y - 0.4*x; }
  // critical points: ∂x: 4x(x²-1) - 0.4 = 0; ∂y: 2y = 0
  // y=0 always. solve 4x³ - 4x - 0.4 = 0
  // approximate: x ≈ -0.95, x≈ -0.10, x≈ 1.06 (the saddle is the middle root)
  function findRoots(){
    const f = x => 4*x*x*x - 4*x - 0.4;
    const roots=[];
    let prev=-2, prevF=f(prev);
    for(let i=1;i<=400;i++){
      const x = -2 + 4*i/400, fx=f(x);
      if(prev*0 < 1 && prevF*fx < 0){
        // bisect
        let lo=prev, hi=x;
        for(let j=0;j<40;j++){
          const m=(lo+hi)/2;
          if(f(lo)*f(m)<0) hi=m; else lo=m;
        }
        roots.push((lo+hi)/2);
      }
      prev=x; prevF=fx;
    }
    return roots;
  }
  const roots = findRoots();
  // valleys are roots where ∂xx J > 0; saddle has ∂xx J < 0
  function Jxx(x){ return 12*x*x - 4; }
  const valleys = roots.filter(x => Jxx(x)>0).map(x=>[x,0,J(x,0)]);
  const saddle = roots.find(x => Jxx(x)<0);
  const saddleVal = J(saddle, 0);

  // contour rendering: sample grid, draw level curves at chosen heights
  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const bend = parseFloat(bS.value);
    const detour = parseFloat(dS.value);
    bO.textContent = `bend = ${bend.toFixed(2)}`;
    dO.textContent = `detour = ${detour.toFixed(2)}`;

    // background: shaded contours by sampling
    const NX=80, NY=60;
    const dx = (xmax-xmin)/NX, dy=(ymax-ymin)/NY;
    let Jmin=Infinity, Jmax=-Infinity;
    const vals = [];
    for(let j=0;j<=NY;j++){
      vals.push([]);
      for(let i=0;i<=NX;i++){
        const x = xmin+i*dx, y=ymin+j*dy, v=J(x,y);
        vals[j].push(v);
        if(v<Jmin) Jmin=v; if(v>Jmax) Jmax=v;
      }
    }
    // contour levels
    const levels = [];
    const Jcap = Math.min(Jmax, 4);
    for(let i=0;i<10;i++){
      levels.push(Jmin + (Jcap-Jmin)*i/9);
    }
    // marching squares lite: for each grid cell, draw a tiny rect colored by avg
    const colors = ["#0e1518","#13201f","#1a2a25","#22322a","#2c3a30","#3a4232","#4a4a30","#5a4f30","#6a5230","#7a5430"];
    for(let j=0;j<NY;j++){
      for(let i=0;i<NX;i++){
        const v = vals[j][i];
        const idx = Math.max(0, Math.min(colors.length-1, Math.floor((v-Jmin)/(Jcap-Jmin)*colors.length)));
        const x0 = xmin+i*dx, y0=ymin+j*dy;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{
          x:sx(x0), y:sy(y0+dy),
          width: sx(x0+dx)-sx(x0), height: sy(y0)-sy(y0+dy),
          fill: colors[idx], "fill-opacity":0.55, stroke:"none"
        }));
      }
    }

    // simple iso-lines as polylines from grid (cheap: sample contour x for each y at a few levels)
    function drawContour(level, color){
      // find segments where J crosses 'level' along grid edges; use polyline approximation
      const segs=[];
      for(let j=0;j<NY;j++){
        for(let i=0;i<NX;i++){
          const corners=[
            [vals[j][i],   xmin+i*dx,     ymin+j*dy],
            [vals[j][i+1], xmin+(i+1)*dx, ymin+j*dy],
            [vals[j+1][i+1],xmin+(i+1)*dx,ymin+(j+1)*dy],
            [vals[j+1][i], xmin+i*dx,     ymin+(j+1)*dy]
          ];
          // for each edge, interpolate intersections with level
          const ints=[];
          for(let e=0;e<4;e++){
            const a=corners[e], b=corners[(e+1)%4];
            if((a[0]-level)*(b[0]-level) < 0){
              const t = (level-a[0])/(b[0]-a[0]);
              ints.push([a[1]+t*(b[1]-a[1]), a[2]+t*(b[2]-a[2])]);
            }
          }
          if(ints.length>=2){
            segs.push([ints[0], ints[1]]);
          }
        }
      }
      for(const [p,q] of segs){
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx(p[0]),y1:sy(p[1]),x2:sx(q[0]),y2:sy(q[1]),
          stroke:color, "stroke-width":1, "stroke-opacity":0.65}));
      }
    }
    // draw a contour at the saddle level
    drawContour(saddleVal, "var(--cyan)");
    // draw a contour above saddle
    drawContour(saddleVal*1.5+0.3, "var(--mute)");

    // valleys
    valleys.forEach((v,i)=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(v[0]),cy:sy(v[1]),r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
      const lbl = SVG("text",{x:sx(v[0])+10,y:sy(v[1])+4,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
      lbl.textContent = `valley ${i===0?"A":"B"}: J=${v[2].toFixed(3)}`;
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
    });
    // saddle
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:sx(saddle),cy:sy(0),r:5,fill:"var(--cyan)"}));
    const slbl = SVG("text",{x:sx(saddle)+10,y:sy(0)-10,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    slbl.textContent = `saddle: J=${saddleVal.toFixed(3)}`;
    svg.appendChild(slbl);

    // path γ from valley A (-0.95-ish, 0) to valley B (1.05-ish, 0)
    // bend offset perpendicular to (B-A) direction; detour adds a hump in y
    const A = valleys[0], B = valleys[1];
    const Npath = 100;
    let maxJ = -Infinity;
    const path=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=Npath;i++){
      const t = i/Npath;
      // straight interp
      let x = A[0] + t*(B[0]-A[0]);
      let y = A[1] + t*(B[1]-A[1]);
      // add bend (perpendicular displacement)
      const px = -(B[1]-A[1]); // perp x
      const py =  (B[0]-A[0]); // perp y
      const pn = Math.hypot(px,py);
      const bumpShape = Math.sin(Math.PI*t); // 0 at endpoints, 1 at midpoint
      x += bend * 0.6 * bumpShape * px / pn;
      y += bend * 0.6 * bumpShape * py / pn;
      // detour: extra bump in +y
      y += detour * 0.8 * Math.sin(Math.PI*t);
      const v = J(x,y);
      if(v>maxJ) maxJ = v;
      path.push([sx(x), sy(y)]);
    }
    const pd = path.map((p,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${p[0]} ${p[1]}`).join(" ");
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:pd, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--pink)", "stroke-width":2.4}));

    out.textContent = `path peak max_t J(γ(t)) = ${maxJ.toFixed(4)}    saddle critical value c = ${saddleVal.toFixed(4)}    excess = ${(maxJ-saddleVal).toFixed(4)}\nMountain-pass: c = inf_γ max_t J(γ(t)) — straighten the path, drop the detour, and the peak approaches c.`;
  }
  bS.addEventListener("input", render);
  dS.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>

5. Isoperimetric inequality

Among planar regions of fixed perimeter $L$, which encloses the largest area? The Greeks knew the answer empirically — the disk — but the rigorous proof took until the 19th century.

$$\boxed{\;4\pi A \;\le\; L^2\;}$$

with equality iff the region is a disk. Equivalently, fixing $L$ gives $A\le L^2/(4\pi)$, fixing $A$ gives $L\ge 2\sqrt{\pi A}$.

Calibration: the variational proof

Setting up the constrained problem: maximise $A(\gamma) = \tfrac12\oint(x\,dy - y\,dx)$ subject to $L(\gamma) = \oint|d\gamma| = L_0$. The Lagrangian is

$$J_\lambda(\gamma) \;=\; A(\gamma) - \lambda\,L(\gamma).$$

Computing the Euler–Lagrange equation along normal variations $h$:

$$\delta A = \int h\,ds, \qquad \delta L = \int \kappa\,h\,ds$$

where $\kappa$ is geodesic curvature. Stationarity for all $h$ forces $\kappa\equiv\lambda$ — constant curvature. The only closed planar curves of constant curvature are circles, and the multiplier $\lambda = 1/r$ ties radius to perimeter via $L=2\pi r$.

The Bonnesen refinement

A stronger inequality due to Bonnesen quantifies how far the region is from a disk:

$$L^2 - 4\pi A \;\ge\; \pi^2(R - r)^2$$

where $R$ is the circumradius and $r$ the inradius of the convex hull. Equality forces $R=r$ (the region is a disk).

Isoperimetric tradeoff — perturb a disk
deform the boundary; watch $A$ vs $L$
a = 0.00
n = 3
Curve $r(\theta) = 1 + a\cos(n\theta)$. At $a=0$ this is the unit disk: $A=\pi$, $L=2\pi$, $4\pi A = L^2 = 4\pi^2$. Any deformation strictly increases $L^2 - 4\pi A$ — that's the isoperimetric defect.

6. Applications: geodesics, minimal surfaces, gauge theory

Almost every fundamental equation of physics — and many of geometry — is the Euler–Lagrange equation for an energy. The dictionary:

FunctionalDomainCritical equationSolutions are
$E(\gamma) = \tfrac12\int g(\dot\gamma,\dot\gamma)\,dt$curves $\gamma\colon[0,1]\to M$$\nabla_{\dot\gamma}\dot\gamma = 0$geodesics
$\mathrm{Area}(\Sigma) = \int_\Sigma\,dA$surfaces $\Sigma\subset\mathbb{R}^3$$H = 0$ (mean curvature)minimal surfaces (soap films)
$\tfrac12\int_\Omega|\Delta u|^2\,dx$$u\in H^2_0(\Omega)$$\Delta^2 u = 0$ (biharmonic)thin elastic plates
$\int|F_A|^2\,d\mathrm{vol}$connections $A$ on $P\to M$$d_A^*F_A = 0$Yang–Mills fields
$\int(\tfrac12|\nabla\phi|^2 + V(\phi))\,dx$scalar fields $\phi$$-\Delta\phi + V'(\phi)=0$solitons, kinks, instantons
$\tfrac12\int|d\phi|^2$maps $\phi\colon M\to N$$\tau(\phi) = 0$ (tension field)harmonic maps

Plateau's problem: the soap-film equation

Given a closed curve $\Gamma\subset\mathbb{R}^3$, find a surface of least area spanning it. For surfaces given as graphs $z = u(x,y)$ over a domain $\Omega$ with $u|_{\partial\Omega}$ prescribed,

$$\mathrm{Area}(u) \;=\; \int_\Omega \sqrt{1+|\nabla u|^2}\,dx\,dy.$$

The Euler–Lagrange equation is

$$\nabla\cdot \frac{\nabla u}{\sqrt{1+|\nabla u|^2}} \;=\; 0,$$

the minimal-surface equation. Solutions include the helicoid, catenoid, Enneper's surface, Costa's surface (1982). Existence in full generality (Plateau, 1931; Douglas, 1939) earned the first Fields Medal.

Yang–Mills: gauge theory as variational

For a principal $G$-bundle $P\to M$ with connection $A$ and curvature $F_A = dA + A\wedge A$, the Yang–Mills functional

$$YM(A) \;=\; \int_M |F_A|^2\,d\mathrm{vol}$$

has critical points satisfying $d_A^* F_A = 0$. The Bianchi identity $d_A F_A = 0$ holds automatically. In four dimensions, self-dual connections $F_A = *F_A$ are absolute minima — Donaldson's theory of smooth $4$-manifolds is built on the moduli space of these instantons.

    const a = -Math.PI/3;
    const x = v[0]*Math.cos(a) - v[1]*Math.sin(a);
    const y = v[0]*Math.sin(a) + v[1]*Math.cos(a);
    const z = v[2];
    // orthographic from +x: project onto (y,z)
    return [cx + R*y, cy - R*z, x]; // returns z-buffer in last slot
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const phi1 = parseFloat(p1S.value);
    const phi2 = parseFloat(p2S.value);
    const dlon = parseFloat(lnS.value);
    p1O.textContent = `φ₁ = ${phi1.toFixed(2)}`;
    p2O.textContent = `φ₂ = ${phi2.toFixed(2)}`;
    lnO.textContent = `Δλ = ${dlon.toFixed(2)}`;

    const lon1 = -dlon/2, lon2 = dlon/2;
    const A = pt(lon1, phi1), B = pt(lon2, phi2);

    // sphere outline
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx,cy,r:R,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1.5}));

    // latitude/longitude grid (for atmosphere)
    for(let lat=-Math.PI/2+0.3; lat<Math.PI/2; lat+=Math.PI/6){
      const pts=[];
      for(let i=0;i<=72;i++){
        const lon = -Math.PI + 2*Math.PI*i/72;
        const p = proj(pt(lon,lat));
        if(p[2] > -0.05) pts.push([p[0],p[1]]);
      }
      if(pts.length>2){
        const d = pts.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-opacity":0.6}));
      }
    }
    for(let lon=-Math.PI; lon<Math.PI; lon+=Math.PI/4){
      const pts=[];
      for(let i=0;i<=36;i++){
        const lat = -Math.PI/2 + Math.PI*i/36;
        const p = proj(pt(lon,lat));
        if(p[2] > -0.05) pts.push([p[0],p[1]]);
      }
      if(pts.length>2){
        const d = pts.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-opacity":0.6}));
      }
    }

    // great-circle from A to B: parametrise as slerp
    const dotAB = A[0]*B[0]+A[1]*B[1]+A[2]*B[2];
    const omega = Math.acos(Math.max(-1,Math.min(1,dotAB)));
    function slerp(t){
      if(omega < 1e-6) return A;
      const s = Math.sin(omega);
      const f1 = Math.sin((1-t)*omega)/s;
      const f2 = Math.sin(t*omega)/s;
      return [f1*A[0]+f2*B[0], f1*A[1]+f2*B[1], f1*A[2]+f2*B[2]];
    }
    const gc=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=120;i++){
      const v = slerp(i/120);
      const p = proj(v);
      gc.push([p[0],p[1],p[2]]);
    }
    // split path on z<0
    {
      let visible=[], all=[];
      for(let i=0;i<gc.length;i++){
        if(gc[i][2] >= -0.02){ visible.push([gc[i][0],gc[i][1]]); }
        else if(visible.length){ all.push(visible); visible=[]; }
      }
      if(visible.length) all.push(visible);
      for(const seg of all){
        const d = seg.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":2.4}));
      }
    }
    const Lgc = omega; // arc-length on unit sphere

    // latitude path: average latitude, arc from lon1 to lon2 at fixed phi_avg
    const phiAvg = 0.5*(phi1+phi2);
    const lat_pts=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=120;i++){
      const lon = lon1 + (lon2-lon1)*i/120;
      const v = pt(lon, phiAvg);
      const p = proj(v);
      lat_pts.push([p[0],p[1],p[2]]);
    }
    {
      let visible=[], all=[];
      for(let i=0;i<lat_pts.length;i++){
        if(lat_pts[i][2] >= -0.02){ visible.push([lat_pts[i][0],lat_pts[i][1]]); }
        else if(visible.length){ all.push(visible); visible=[]; }
      }
      if(visible.length) all.push(visible);
      for(const seg of all){
        const d = seg.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?"M":"L"}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(" ");
        svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d, fill:"none", stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2,"stroke-dasharray":"4,4"}));
      }
    }
    // length of latitude path
    const Llat = Math.cos(phiAvg)*Math.abs(lon2-lon1);

    // endpoints
    const pA = proj(A), pB = proj(B);
    if(pA[2]>-0.02){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:pA[0],cy:pA[1],r:5,fill:"var(--green)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pA[0]+8, y:pA[1]+4,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--green)"});
      t.textContent = "A"; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    if(pB[2]>-0.02){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:pB[0],cy:pB[1],r:5,fill:"var(--pink)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:pB[0]+8, y:pB[1]+4,"font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)"});
      t.textContent = "B"; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // legend
    const xL = W-180, yL = 30;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xL,y1:yL,x2:xL+18,y2:yL,stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":2.4}));
    const t1 = SVG("text",{x:xL+24, y:yL+4,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    t1.textContent = `great circle  L=${Lgc.toFixed(4)}`; svg.appendChild(t1);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xL,y1:yL+22,x2:xL+18,y2:yL+22,stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2,"stroke-dasharray":"4,4"}));
    const t2 = SVG("text",{x:xL+24, y:yL+26,"font-size":12, fill:"var(--cyan)"});
    t2.textContent = `latitude arc   L=${Llat.toFixed(4)}`; svg.appendChild(t2);

    out.textContent = `Geodesic (great circle) length = ${Lgc.toFixed(4)}    latitude-arc length = ${Llat.toFixed(4)}    geodesic shorter by ${(100*(Llat-Lgc)/Llat).toFixed(2)}%`;
  }
  p1S.addEventListener("input", render);
  p2S.addEventListener("input", render);
  lnS.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>

Connections

Variational methods sit at the centre of analysis: they convert questions about the existence of solutions to PDEs into questions about the geometry of energy landscapes on infinite-dimensional spaces. Modern frontiers include the Allen–Cahn / Ginzburg–Landau passage from energies to mean-curvature flow, Almgren–Pitts min-max for minimal surfaces in arbitrary dimension, concentration-compactness at critical Sobolev exponents, and the calculus of variations on metric-measure spaces.

  • Sobolev spaces & distributions — variational Poisson — the linear template before nonlinearity is added.
  • Partial differential equations — Lax–Milgram & regularity — variational solutions promoted to classical ones via elliptic regularity.
  • Functional analysis — weak topology & Banach–Alaoglu — the compactness engine behind the direct method.
  • Riemannian geometry — geodesics, harmonic maps, the second variation and Jacobi fields.
  • Morse theory — saddle critical points organise into a chain complex; mountain-pass is the index-1 case.
